rulings.Case Title: PHILIP THOMASCHIMA VS. ESURANCE INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 8, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED MOTION TO STRIKE. Off Calendar, as moot in view of ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP THOMASCHIMA VS. ESURANCE INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 8, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for B&P 17200 unfair business practices. Plaintiff may not plead around the absolute bar to relief created by Insurance Code section 790.03 by recasting the cause of action as one for unfair competition. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action to plead fraud with specificity. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to first and second causes of action in view of Insurance Code section 11580.2 requirements. No reasonable probability that defect could be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BARRY LEE VS. CHIA JI NEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 8, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT CHIA NEI Notice Of Motion For Attorney's Fees. Motion for attorney fees denied. CC section 1717(b)(2) bars recovery of attorney fees where on action has been voluntarily dismissed. However, defendant is the prevailing party for purposes of costs in that he remained in possession and avoided payment of alleged due rent and property taxes. CCP section 1032 and section 1033.5. Costs awarded to defendant in the amount of $738.50. =(302/CWW)
